name: DigiCert REST API
what_it_does: Enterprise PKI and SSL/TLS certificate management REST API for issuing, renewing, revoking, and managing SSL certificates, code signing certificates, and S/MIME at enterprise scale.
use_cases: Automating SSL certificate issuance and renewal in CI/CD pipelines via API
Managing certificate lifecycle (issuance, renewal, revocation) across thousands of domains
Issuing code signing certificates for software release automation agents
Monitoring certificate expiration dates and triggering proactive renewal
Managing client authentication certificates for zero-trust access control
not_for: Free SSL certificates (use Let's Encrypt/cert-manager instead)
Internal PKI without external trust requirements (use HashiCorp Vault PKI or CFSSL)
Single-domain personal certificates where pricing is disproportionate
best_when: An agent manages enterprise SSL certificate operations at scale where OV/EV trust level, code signing, or S/MIME are required.
avoid_when: You need free DV certificates — Let's Encrypt via ACME protocol is the right choice.

auth.notes: API keys with configurable permission levels from DigiCert CertCentral dashboard. Division-level access controls for enterprise multi-team deployments. Keys passed via X-DC-DEVKEY header.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; API access included with certificate purchase
pricing.paid_tiers: DV SSL from ~$50/year; OV from ~$150/year; EV from ~$300/year; code signing from ~$600/year
pricing.notes: API access is free with an account; costs are per certificate issued. Enterprise volume discounts available. Competitively priced vs GlobalSign and Sectigo for mid-market.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; standard API rate limiting per account

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: DigiCert Developer Center has comprehensive REST API documentation. Error responses are JSON with error codes and messages. Domain validation status can be queried to track progress.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Certificate orders are identifiable by order ID. Duplicate order detection based on CSR/domain. Webhook notifications for order status changes.
known_agent_gotchas: OV/EV validation is a human process (days, not minutes) — agents cannot fully automate issuance
Domain validation (DV) can be automated but requires DNS or file-based validation steps
Certificate issuance is async — order must be placed and polled for completion
CSR generation must happen before order creation — agents need crypto library integration

security_score_components.security_notes: WebTrust-audited CA with the strictest CA/B Forum compliance. API keys can be scoped by division. Certificate private keys generated client-side — DigiCert never sees private keys. SOC2 and ISO27001 certified.
